change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value: The 6 year post-earthquake reconstruction programme started in May 2016 at the completion of DFID's humanitarian operations in country.The project in its initial year has provided an array of support to meet immediate priorities of both the GoN's National Reconstruction Authority and vulnerable populations residing in some of the most affected earthquake areas. For example DFID is supporting the Housing Recovery and Reconstruction Platform (HRRP) for coordination and information management of the housing reconstruction sector; activated water and trail related programming in Gorkha, Rasuwa, Dhading and Nuwakot (DFID's focal districts); providing technical assistance to the NRA; supporting a programme on durable solutions for those displaced by the earthquake; contributed resources to the GoN's housing reconstruction cash grant programme for families whose houses have been completely or majorly damaged; enrolling of individuals to the GoN's housing grant programme in 11 most affected districts; geo-hazard assessments of over 500 vulnerable sites; housing damage survey in additional 17 moderately affected districts amongst others.DFID Reconstruction Prgramme team is based in Kathmandu (headead by the Reconstruction Team Leader, Senior Reconstruction Advisor, Programme Manager and a Programme Officer); DFID also has Field Officers based in Gorkha,Dhading, Rasuwa and Nuwakot who are the main district focal points and lead on monitoring activities of DFID's work in the districts

snapshot.executing_agency: National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Authority
snapshot.implementing_agency: CARE Nepal, United Nations Development Programme, People in Need, Action Contre La Faim, UNOPS, International Organization for Migration, World Food Programme

en.full: Post Earthquake Reconstruction Programme in Nepal - Building Back Better

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
